mapper.select: SELECT , FROM users
SELECT FROM users
SELECT FROM users
SELECT FROM users
SELECT id, name, email FROM users
SELECT id, name, email FROM users
SELECT FROM users

SELECT FROM users
AND status = #{status}
SELECT , status, department FROM users

SELECT FROM users
AND status = #{status}
SELECT , FROM orders o JOIN users u ON
SELECT id, name, status FROM users
mapper.sql: id, name, email, status, created_at, updated_at
id, order_no, amount, status as order_status, created_at as order_date
mapper.insert:
INSERT INTO users (id, name, email, status, department, created_at)
(#{user.id}, #{user.name}, #{user.email},
#{user.status} 'active' ,
#{user.department} 'Default' , NOW())
mapper.select:
SELECT FROM users WHERE status = 'active' UNION ALL SELECT FROM users WHERE status = 'pending' UNION ALL SELECT FROM users WHERE status = 'inactive'
ORDER BY ${sortField}
mapper.sql: CASE id, name, email, status, department, created_at, updated_at id, name, email id, name, status id, name END
mapper.select:
SELECT FROM users
AND status = #{status}

SELECT u.id, u.name, (SELECT FROM users u2 WHERE u2.id = u.id) as user_details FROM users u
AND u.status = #{status}
SELECT id, name, email FROM users WHERE id = #{id}
SELECT id, name, email FROM users ORDER BY created_at DESC
SELECT status, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM users GROUP BY status
SELECT FROM users
SELECT FROM users
SELECT FROM users

SELECT id, name FROM users
WHERE status = #{status}

SELECT id, name FROM users
ORDER BY ${sortField}

SELECT * FROM users
WHERE name LIKE CONCAT('%', #{name}, '%')
AND email LIKE CONCAT('%', #{email}, '%')

SELECT id, name, status FROM users WHERE status = 'active' AND id IN
#{id}

SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE (id, status) IN
(#{p.id}, #{p.status})

SELECT id, name FROM users
WHERE status = 'active'
WHERE status = 'inactive'
WHERE 1=1

SELECT id, name, status FROM users
WHERE 1=1
WHERE status = 'active'
WHERE status = 'public'
WHERE status = 'active'

SELECT id, name FROM users
,#{sortField}
SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE name LIKE #{pattern}
SELECT id, name, email FROM users WHERE email LIKE #{emailPattern}
SELECT o.id, o.order_no, u.name as user_name FROM orders o JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.id WHERE o.status = 'active'
SELECT o.id, o.order_no, u.name as user_name, p.name as product_name FROM orders o JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.id JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.id WHERE o.status = 'active'

SELECT o.id, u.name as user_name, p.name as product_name, c.name as category_name FROM orders o JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.id JOIN products p ON o.product_id = p.id JOIN categories c ON p.category_id = c.id WHERE o.status = 'active'
SELECT o.id, o.order_no, u.name as user_name FROM orders o LEFT JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.id WHERE o.status = 'pending'
SELECT id, name FROM users u WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders o WHERE o.user_id = u.id)
SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE id IN (SELECT user_id FROM orders WHERE status = 'active')
SELECT id, name, (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE user_id = users.id) as order_count FROM users
SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE status = 'active' UNION SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE status = 'pending'
SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE status = 'active' UNION SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE status = 'pending' UNION SELECT id, name FROM users WHERE status = 'inactive'
SELECT id, name, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY status ORDER BY created_at DESC) as rn FROM users
SELECT id, name, amount, RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY status ORDER BY amount DESC) as rank FROM orders
SELECT id, name, amount, SUM(amount) OVER (PARTITION BY status) as status_total FROM orders
SELECT id, name, status FROM users ORDER BY ${orderField} ${orderDir}
SELECT status, COUNT(*) as cnt FROM ( SELECT status FROM users WHERE created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days' ) recent_users GROUP BY status
SELECT id, name FROM users

SELECT id, name, status FROM users
WHERE department = #{department}
WHERE 1=1
WHERE status = 'active'
WHERE status = 'public'
SELECT , FROM users
SELECT id, name, CASE WHEN status = 'active' THEN 'Active User' WHEN status = 'pending' THEN 'Pending User' ELSE 'Unknown' END as user_type FROM users

SELECT id, name FROM users u
WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM orders o WHERE o.user_id = u.id)
SELECT u1.id, u1.name as user1, u2.name as referrer FROM users u1 LEFT JOIN users u2 ON u1.referrer_id = u2.id

SELECT user_id, COUNT(*) as order_count FROM orders GROUP BY user_id
HAVING COUNT(*) >= #{minCount}

SELECT DISTINCT status FROM users
AND name LIKE CONCAT('%', #{name}, '%')

SELECT COUNT(*) FROM users
AND status = #{status}

SELECT o.id, u.name FROM orders o JOIN users u ON o.user_id = u.id WHERE o.status = 'active' AND o.id IN
#{oid}

SELECT id, name FROM users
OR status = 'active'
OR status = 'pending'

WITH RECURSIVE org_tree AS ( SELECT id, name, parent_id, 1 as level FROM departments WHERE parent_id IS NULL UNION ALL SELECT d.id, d.name, d.parent_id, ot.level + 1 FROM departments d JOIN org_tree ot ON d.parent_id = ot.id ) SELECT * FROM org_tree

SELECT id, name, status FROM users
ORDER BY created_at DESC
ORDER BY name ASC
ORDER BY id ASC

SELECT id, name FROM users
WHERE status = 'active'
WHERE status = 'pending'
WHERE 1=1
LIMIT #{limit}

SELECT id, name FROM users
WHERE status IN
WHERE status = 'active' AND id IN
#{id}

SELECT FROM users
AND status = #{status}
mapper.update:
UPDATE orders
status = #{status}, amount = #{amount},
AND id IN
#{oid}
mapper.select:
SELECT id, name, email FROM users
WHERE status = 'active'
ORDER BY created_at DESC
LIMIT #{limit}

SELECT id, name FROM users
AND name = CONCAT(#{firstName}, ' ', #{lastName})
AND name LIKE CONCAT(#{firstName}, '%')

SELECT id, name FROM users
(id, status) IN
(#{f.id}, #{f.status})

SELECT FROM users
AND status = 'active'
ORDER BY created_at DESC

SELECT id, name FROM users
AND name LIKE #{namePattern}

SELECT id, name FROM users
,${sortField}

SELECT id, name, status, department FROM users
WHERE department = #{dept}
WHERE 1=1
WHERE status = 'active'
WHERE status = 'public'
ORDER BY created_at DESC

SELECT id, name, email, status FROM users
AND name LIKE CONCAT('%', #{name}, '%')
AND email LIKE CONCAT('%', #{email}, '%')
AND status IN
#{s}

SELECT FROM users
AND status = #{status}

SELECT id, name, email, status, department FROM users
status = #{status} AND name = #{name} AND department = #{dept},

SELECT id, name, email, status, department, created_at FROM users
WHERE status = 'active' AND department = 'Admin'
WHERE status = 'active' AND department = 'Manager'
WHERE status = 'active'
WHERE status = 'inactive'
WHERE status = 'pending'
WHERE department = #{dept}
WHERE 1=1
ORDER BY name
ORDER BY created_at
ORDER BY status
ORDER BY id
mapper.update:
UPDATE users
name = #{name},
email = #{email},
status = #{status},
department = #{profile.department},
title = #{profile.title},
WHERE id = #{id}

UPDATE users u
u.name = #{user.name},
u.email = #{user.email},
u.department = #{user.profile.department},
u.title = #{user.profile.title},
u.metadata = #{user.profile.metadata},
u.status = #{status},
AND u.id IN
#{id}
AND u.department_id IN
#{did}
AND u.status != #{excludeStatus}
mapper.select:
SELECT id, name, email, status, department FROM users
status = #{status} AND department = #{dept} AND
status = #{status} AND
name = #{name} AND
email = #{email},
